events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes double-tapped breakers that are non-listed. This is a clear code violation under NEC Article 408. Double-tapping (putting two conductors under one breaker lug) is only allowed if the breaker is specifically listed and labeled for that use. Non-listed double-tapped breakers are non-compliant and present a fire/overheating hazard.

Decision: fail
Risk: high (fire hazard from loose connections/overheating)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: non-compliant, installed-defective
Percent complete: maybe 85-90% since rough is complete but not closed out
Value: 0 (no numeric reading requested)
Confidence: high, 0.95
Findings: double-tapped breakers not listed for multiple conductors
Actions: remove double taps, install listed breakers or use approved pigtail/splice
References: NEC Art. 408, 110.26, 200.4

    "Remove double-tapped conductors from affected breakers",
    "Install breakers listed and labeled for double-tap termination, or pigtail conductors under a listed splice in the gutter and land a single conductor per lug",

    "Re-inspect panelboard makeup before energizing"
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC Art. 408 requires panelboard terminations to comply with manufacturer listing and instructions; a breaker not listed for multiple conductors per lug cannot accept two conductors. This creates a loose-connection/overheating hazard and is non-compliant at rough-complete stage.",
